label: Grafana Loki
description: Query and stream logs from a Grafana Loki instance using LogQL.

form.title: Connect Grafana Loki
form.subtitle: Point Anton at your Loki endpoint to query and tail log streams.

methods.label: Loki URL (no auth)
methods.description: Paste your Loki base URL. Use this for local or open instances that require no authentication.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Loki without authentication

The simplest path — just paste the base URL of your Loki instance.

### Steps

1. Identify the base URL of your Loki instance, e.g. `http://localhost:3100` or `https://loki.internal.example.com`.
2. If you are running Loki via Grafana Cloud or behind a reverse proxy, confirm the path prefix (usually none at the root).
3. Paste the URL into the **Loki URL** field below and click **Submit**.
4. Anton will verify connectivity by hitting the `/ready` endpoint.

### Notes

- Do not include a trailing slash.
- If your instance is behind a firewall, ensure the host running Anton can reach it on the correct port.
- For instances that require authentication, use one of the other methods instead.

fields.name: loki_url
fields.label: Loki URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Loki instance. No trailing slash.

methods.label: Basic Auth (username + password)
methods.description: Authenticate with a username and password or API key. Works with Grafana Cloud Loki and any Loki instance protected by HTTP basic auth.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Loki with Basic Auth

Used for Grafana Cloud Loki or self-hosted Loki instances behind an HTTP basic-auth proxy.

### Grafana Cloud steps

1. Open [grafana.com](https://grafana.com) → **My Account** → your stack → **Loki** section.
2. Note the **URL** shown (e.g. `https://logs-prod-us-central1.grafana.net`).
3. Under **Password / API Key**, click **Generate now** or use an existing API key.
4. Your **User** is the numeric ID shown next to the URL (e.g. `123456`).
5. Paste the URL, user ID, and API key/password into the fields below.

### Self-hosted steps

1. Find the Loki base URL from your deployment config or reverse-proxy config.
2. Enter the username and password configured in your proxy (e.g. nginx `htpasswd` or Loki's own auth middleware).

### Notes

- For Grafana Cloud, the password field accepts an API token, not your grafana.com account password.
- Credentials are stored locally and never sent to Anton's servers.

fields.name: loki_url
fields.label: Loki URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Loki instance or Grafana Cloud Loki endpoint.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username / User ID
fields.description: For Grafana Cloud this is the numeric User ID shown on your stack's Loki details page.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password / API Key
fields.description: Your HTTP basic auth password or a Grafana Cloud API key/token.

methods.label: Multi-tenant (X-Scope-OrgID)
methods.description: For multi-tenant Loki deployments. Sends an X-Scope-OrgID header on every request to target a specific tenant. Optionally combine with basic auth.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to a multi-tenant Loki deployment

Loki's multi-tenancy mode requires every request to carry an `X-Scope-OrgID` header identifying the tenant. This method lets you set that header, optionally alongside basic auth credentials.

### Steps

1. Confirm your Loki deployment runs with `auth_enabled: true` in `loki-config.yaml`.
2. Obtain the tenant (org) ID for the namespace you want to query — this is typically set by your Loki operator or platform team.
3. Enter the Loki base URL and the **Org / Tenant ID** below.
4. If the endpoint also requires HTTP basic auth, fill in the **Username** and **Password** fields too; otherwise leave them blank.
5. Click **Submit**. Anton will inject the `X-Scope-OrgID` header on every API call.

### Notes

- The tenant ID is case-sensitive.
- To query multiple tenants, create a separate Anton connector for each.
- If `auth_enabled: false`, Loki ignores the header — use the **Loki URL (no auth)** method instead.
- See the [Loki multi-tenancy docs](https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/operations/authentication/) for full details.

fields.name: loki_url
fields.label: Loki URL
fields.description: Base URL of your multi-tenant Loki instance.

fields.name: org_id
fields.label: Org / Tenant ID
fields.description: Value sent as the X-Scope-OrgID header. Must match a tenant configured in your Loki deployment.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username (optional)
fields.description: Only required if your Loki endpoint is also protected by HTTP basic auth.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password (optional)
fields.description: Only required if your Loki endpoint is also protected by HTTP basic auth.
